title: Schoenia macivorii
taxonomy: Schoenia macivorii is a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of flowering plants in the tribe Gnaphalieae, which is a group of daisy-like plants native to Australia. The genus Schoenia is further divided into several species, including Schoenia macivorii, which is a rare and endangered species. Schoenia macivorii is native to Western Australia, where it can be found growing in sandy soils. The classification of Schoenia macivorii reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Schoenia macivorii, also known as Blue Devil or Devil's Weed, is a plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used by the indigenous people of Western Australia, the plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Schoenia macivorii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its therapeutic benefits.
